And, whereas, the objections and suggestions were not received from the public on the said proposals in the (Tent ral Gov eminent; Now: therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by Section 6 of the Export (Quality Control and Inspectioni Act. 1963 (22 of l 963), the Central Government, after consulting the Export Inspection Council, hereby makes the fol lowni;y amendments in the Order of the Government of India in the erstwhile Ministry of Commerce No. SO. 729(E) dated 21st August, 1995 relating to the Fresh, Frozen and Processed Fish and Fishery Products which shall take effect on the date 01‘ its publication in the Official Gazette, namely :A In the said Order. in Schedulc~1 after clause (h), the following clause shall be inserted, natitcly ;, < "(1) I The level of benzo(a) pyrcnc in tnuscle meat of smoked fish and smoked fishery prodtlcts shall not excecd i pph (wet weight), M The level of ben7e(a) pyrene in muscle meat offish other than smoked fish shall not exceed 2 ppb (wet weight I and: t .The l€V el of herizo(a) pyrene in bivalve niolluscs, crustacean and cephalopods ot her than smoked shall not exceed 5 pph (wet weight)” [F.Nofi/Z/Ztttthhl(U71): MVPC.
